purpose: Implement and enforce the core-boundary/provider-plugin architecture described in docs/decisions/ADR-core-boundary-and-provider-plugins.md so Ananta can grow integrations such as Blender, KiCad, FreeCAD, workflow automation providers, MCP, Evolver, DeerFlow and worker backends without turning the Hub/Core into a Medusa.
non_goals: Do not remove existing useful integrations just because they are not perfect yet.
Do not build a heavy plugin marketplace or dynamic code-loading system in this track.
Do not move policy, approval, audit or task state into providers.
Do not make optional providers required for local-dev startup or core tests.
Do not allow provider-specific code to bypass Hub-owned capabilities and policy checks.
success_criteria: Provider-specific logic lives behind explicit provider interfaces and factories/registries.
Core startup and core tests pass with optional providers disabled or missing.
Domain graph providers output provider-neutral DomainGraphArtifact objects.
Workflow automation providers output provider-neutral WorkflowIntegrationRunArtifact objects.
Worker execution providers output provider-neutral WorkerResult/Patch/Artifact contracts.
Research/evolution providers output provider-neutral run/proposal artifacts.
Release gate detects obvious provider-specific imports leaking into core modules.
Docs explain how to add a provider without touching core orchestration.

tasks.title: Provider coupling inventory add
acceptance_criteria: Inventory lists current direct references/imports to provider-specific systems in core-ish modules.
Inventory covers Blender/FreeCAD/KiCad, workflow automation, worker execution backends, MCP, Evolver, DeerFlow and Git provider integrations where present.
Inventory classifies each coupling as acceptable adapter code, suspicious core leak or deferred/unknown.
Inventory proposes target provider family for each suspicious coupling.

tasks.title: Core package boundary definition add
acceptance_criteria: Document defines which packages/modules count as Core, Provider Interface, Provider Implementation and Client Adapter.
Document explicitly maps existing modules into those zones where feasible.
Tests or static checks can read the boundary configuration.
Boundary definition is conservative and does not require immediate full migration.

tasks.title: Provider interface package skeleton add
acceptance_criteria: Adds stable interfaces/protocols for provider registration and health/status reporting.
Interfaces are provider-neutral and do not import n8n, Blender, KiCad, FreeCAD, OpenCode or similar provider-specific packages.
Includes common ProviderDescriptor fields: provider_id, provider_family, capabilities, risk_class, enabled_by_default=false.
Tests verify interface objects can be imported without optional provider dependencies.

tasks.title: Generic provider registry add
acceptance_criteria: Registry supports registering provider descriptors and provider factories by family.
Unknown providers are disabled/unavailable by default.
Registry can report missing optional dependency as degraded instead of failing core startup.
Registry supports injected mock providers for tests.
Tests cover disabled provider, missing dependency, registered mock and unknown provider.

tasks.title: DomainGraphProvider interface add
acceptance_criteria: Defines provider-neutral interface for graph ingestion from Blender/FreeCAD/KiCad-like sources.
Interface returns DomainGraphArtifact-compatible payload plus provenance/warnings.
No Blender/KiCad/FreeCAD-specific import appears in core/interface module.
Mock provider test proves core can consume DomainGraphArtifact without real domain tool installed.

tasks.title: WorkflowProvider interface align with workflow adapter todo
acceptance_criteria: Defines provider-neutral workflow provider interface compatible with workflow automation adapter layer.
n8n/generic webhook provider can later implement the interface without changing core.
Interface supports dry-run, callback correlation and provider status.
Tests use mock provider only.

tasks.title: WorkerExecutionProvider interface align with ananta-worker
acceptance_criteria: Defines provider-neutral worker execution interface for ananta-worker, OpenCode, Aider-like and shell-backed executors.
Interface uses WorkerJob, context bundle, allowed capabilities/tools and expected output schema.
Provider cannot execute without Hub-issued policy/capability context.
Tests prove mock worker provider returns provider-neutral result artifacts.

tasks.title: Provider-neutral artifact contract inventory add
acceptance_criteria: Document maps each provider family to neutral artifact contracts: DomainGraphArtifact, WorkflowIntegrationRunArtifact, WorkerResultArtifact, PatchArtifact, EvolutionProposalArtifact.
Document identifies missing schemas or schema gaps.
Document states provider-specific raw payloads must be stored behind metadata/raw_payload fields only when safe and redacted.

tasks.title: Provider artifact redaction utility add
acceptance_criteria: Utility redacts secret-looking keys and configured secret refs from provider payloads before logging/artifact storage.
Utility is provider-neutral and can be used by workflow/domain/worker providers.
Tests cover nested dict/list redaction and no-op safe payloads.

tasks.title: Provider provenance metadata standard add
acceptance_criteria: Defines standard provider provenance fields: provider_id, provider_family, provider_version, external_ref, source_ref, run_id, trace_id.
Provenance helper can be embedded in artifact metadata consistently.
Tests verify deterministic provenance construction and safe missing fields.

tasks.title: Core forbidden import checker add
acceptance_criteria: Checker flags provider-specific imports or strings in configured core modules when they are not allowlisted.
Checker starts with warning/report mode to avoid breaking migration prematurely.
Checker has allowlist for legitimate provider interface modules.
Tests include synthetic violation for Blender/n8n/OpenCode-style imports.

tasks.title: Release gate provider boundary check add
acceptance_criteria: Release gate can run provider boundary checker in report or strict mode.
Core release gate passes without optional provider dependencies installed.
Failure message points to ADR-core-boundary-and-provider-plugins.md and provider interface package.
Tests cover report mode and strict mode.

tasks.title: Disabled optional provider startup test add
acceptance_criteria: Smoke test verifies core app/import path starts with optional providers disabled/missing.
Test explicitly covers workflow automation, domain graph and external worker providers disabled.
Test fails if optional provider dependency becomes required for local-dev/core startup.

tasks.title: Provider implementation layout docs add
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain where provider interfaces, provider implementations, schemas and tests belong.
Docs include minimal mock provider example.
Docs include rules for disabled-by-default, dry-run, redaction and policy gating.
Docs warn against direct imports in core modules.

tasks.title: Existing integration tracks cross-reference ADR add
acceptance_criteria: Workflow automation todo references provider boundary ADR and generic provider registry.
Planning/blueprint cleanup references provider artifact contracts where domain graph providers are relevant.
ADR relationship section remains accurate.

tasks.title: Medusa risk checklist add
acceptance_criteria: Checklist helps evaluate every new integration before implementation.
Checklist asks: can this provider be disabled without breaking core? does it have a neutral artifact? does it bypass policy? does it require optional deps?
Checklist is referenced by provider plugin guide and ADR.

execution_order_summary: Add provider-neutral interfaces and registry with mock providers.
Add boundary checker and disabled-provider startup smoke tests.
Document provider plugin layout and Medusa risk checklist.
Cross-reference existing workflow, domain graph, worker and planning tracks.
summary_notes: This track protects Ananta from Medusa growth while still allowing many integrations.
The highest-value early tasks are boundary inventory, provider interfaces, registry, forbidden-import checker and disabled-provider startup smoke.
Do not overbuild dynamic plugin loading yet; simple explicit provider registries are enough.
Blender/KiCad/FreeCAD, workflow automation, external workers and research/evolution providers should all pass through this architecture.
